sections.title: Overview & Goals
sections.promptHint: Describe what this PRD covers: feature name, the team that owns it, the business goal it serves, and how success is measured. Include a one-sentence 'north star' — the single most important outcome.
sections.ragQuery: feature goals objectives success metrics business goal

sections.title: Problem Statement
sections.promptHint: Describe the user problem in the user's own language. Include: who is affected, what they're trying to do, what gets in their way today, and the cost of that friction. Avoid describing the solution here.
sections.ragQuery: problem user pain friction challenge current state

sections.title: User Stories
sections.promptHint: Write 3–8 user stories in the format: 'As a [user type], I want to [action], so that [benefit].' Follow each story with 2–5 acceptance criteria as a numbered checklist.
sections.ragQuery: user stories use cases user flows scenarios

sections.title: Functional Requirements
sections.promptHint: List specific, testable behaviors the system must have. Number each requirement. Use 'shall' for mandatory, 'should' for preferred. Each requirement must be independently testable. Include error states and edge cases.

sections.title: Non-Functional Requirements
sections.promptHint: Define performance, security, accessibility, reliability, and scalability requirements. Each must have a measurable target (e.g. 'p95 latency under 200ms', 'WCAG 2.1 AA compliant').

sections.title: Out of Scope
sections.promptHint: Explicitly list what this PRD does NOT cover. This prevents scope creep and clarifies what will be addressed in future iterations. Be specific.

sections.title: Success Metrics
sections.promptHint: Define 3–6 KPIs that will be used to measure whether this feature succeeded. Each metric should have: name, current baseline (if known), target, and measurement method.
sections.ragQuery: metrics KPIs success measurement baseline target

sections.title: Open Questions
sections.promptHint: List unresolved design or product decisions that need input before build begins. For each: the question, why it matters, and who needs to answer it.
